name: Chart.js (Simple JavaScript Charts)

performance.notes: Canvas-based rendering; fast for typical datasets (< 10k points); streaming updates supported

agent_readiness.notes: Most popular JavaScript charting library by downloads. Simple, clean API for standard chart types: line, bar, radar, doughnut, pie, polar area, bubble, scatter. Canvas-based rendering. Responsive by default. Plugin system (zoom, annotation). Streaming updates for real-time charts. TypeScript types. Works without frameworks or with React (react-chartjs-2), Vue, Angular wrappers. Large community, easy to customize. Limited chart types vs Echarts/Plotly.

gotchas: Limited built-in chart types (no treemap, sankey, sunburst)
Complex custom chart types require significant effort
Performance degrades with thousands of data points
best_when: Standard chart types (line, bar, doughnut) in web apps
avoid_when: Specialized chart types (use Plotly or ECharts)
Very large datasets (use deck.gl or WebGL alternatives)
summary: Chart.js is the most downloaded JavaScript charting library — simple, clean API for line, bar, radar, doughnut, pie, bubble, and scatter charts. Canvas-based with responsive defaults. Streaming updates for real-time data. TypeScript types. Large ecosystem with React (react-chartjs-2), Vue, and Angular wrappers. Limited chart types but excellent for standard charts. Most accessible entry point for JavaScript charting.
